info.title: Output.ai API
info.description: API for managing and executing Output.ai workflows

ErrorResponse.description: API error body (WorkflowNotFoundError, UnsupportedWorkflowError, WorkflowExecutionTimedOutError, WorkflowNotCompletedError, CatalogNotAvailableError, or server error)

error.description: Error type name (e.g. WorkflowNotFoundError, UnsupportedWorkflowError, CatalogNotAvailableError)

workflowId.description: Workflow ID when the error is tied to a specific execution (e.g. timeout)

path.description: Absolute path to the workflow file

aliases.description: Alternative names that resolve to this workflow

TraceInfo.description: An object with information about the trace generated by the execution

local.description: Absolute path to local trace file, omitted if not saved locally

remote.description: Remote trace location (e.g., S3 URI), omitted if not saved remotely

TraceData.description: Trace data containing workflow execution details

source.description: Indicates trace was fetched from remote storage

runId.description: The specific run id this trace belongs to
The specific run id this trace belongs to

localPath.description: Absolute path to local trace file

runId.description: The specific run id for this execution

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of completion, or null if still running

runId.description: The specific run id for this execution

startedAt.description: An epoch timestamp representing when the workflow started

completedAt.description: An epoch timestamp representing when the workflow ended

runId.description: The specific run id for this execution

input.description: The original input passed to the workflow, null if unavailable

output.description: The result of workflow, null if workflow failed

error.description: Error message if workflow failed, null otherwise

errorDetails.description: Structured failure details if the workflow failed, null otherwise

message.description: Friendly failure message (from the underlying application error)

retryable.description: Whether Temporal flagged the failure retryable; null if unknown

activityId.description: Failing activity key ("workflow#step"); null if no activity failed

cause.description: Sanitized error cause chain (name/message per level, no stack)

runId.description: The specific run id the input was read from

input.description: The first input argument the workflow was started with, null if unavailable

stepName.description: The name of the step to reset after

runId.description: The run ID of the new execution created by the reset

BadRequest.description: Invalid request body, query, or pagination token

NotFound.description: Workflow execution, workflow type, or catalog not found

RequestTimeout.description: Synchronous execution timed out before workflow completed

FailedDependency.description: Workflow dependency failed (e.g. workflow not in a terminal state, or workflow type is unsupported by the selected catalog)

Conflict.description: Workflow run is in a state that conflicts with the requested operation (e.g. operating on an already-terminal run, or resetting to an incomplete step).

ServiceUnavailable.description: Catalog workflow unavailable (worker not running or still starting). Retry-After header may be set.

InternalServerError.description: Internal server error (e.g. Temporal connection failure)

get.summary: Check if the API is ready to answer requests

post.description: Executes a workflow and waits for it to complete before returning the result

workflowName.description: The name of the workflow to execute

input.description: The payload to send to the workflow

workflowId.description: (Optional) The workflowId to use. Must be unique

catalog.description: The catalog (Temporal task queue) to route the execution to. Falls back to the default catalog.

taskQueue.description: Deprecated alias for `catalog`. If both are sent, `catalog` wins.

timeout.description: (Optional) The max time to wait for the execution, defaults to 30s

workflowName.description: The name of the workflow to execute

input.description: The payload to send to the workflow

workflowId.description: (Optional) The workflowId to use. Must be unique

catalog.description: The catalog (Temporal task queue) to route the execution to. Falls back to the default catalog.

taskQueue.description: Deprecated alias for `catalog`. If both are sent, `catalog` wins.

workflowId.description: The id of the started workflow

runId.description: The first execution's run id for this workflow

get.description: Returns the status of the latest run for the given workflow. To pin a specific run, use `/workflow/{id}/runs/{rid}/status`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of workflow to retrieve the status

get.summary: Get workflow execution status for a specific run

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of workflow

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The specific run id to target

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The specific run id to stop

patch.description: Stops the latest run of the given workflow. The returned `runId` reflects the run at describe-time and may differ from the cancelled run if a new execution started concurrently. Deprecated; use `PATCH /workflow/{id}/runs/{rid}/stop` to pin a specific run. Scheduled for removal after 2026-07-16.

post.summary: Terminate a specific workflow run (force stop)
post.description: Force terminates a workflow run. Unlike stop/cancel, terminate immediately stops the run without allowing cleanup.

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The specific run id to terminate

post.description: Force terminates the latest run. Deprecated; use `POST /workflow/{id}/runs/{rid}/terminate` to target a specific run. Scheduled for removal after 2026-07-16.

post.summary: Reset a specific workflow run to re-run from after a completed step
post.description: Resets a pinned workflow run to the point after a completed step, creating a new run that replays from that point. The current execution is terminated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The workflow ID to reset

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The specific run id to reset

post.description: Resets the latest run. Deprecated; use `POST /workflow/{id}/runs/{rid}/reset` to target a specific run. Scheduled for removal after 2026-07-16.

get.summary: Return the result of a workflow (latest run)
get.description: Returns the result of the latest run for the given workflow. To pin a specific run, use `/workflow/{id}/runs/{rid}/result`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of workflow to retrieve the result

get.summary: Return the result of a specific workflow run

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The specific run id to target

get.summary: Return the original input of a workflow (latest run)
get.description: Returns the original input passed to the latest run of the given workflow. Works for workflows in any state, including running. To pin a specific run, use `/workflow/{id}/runs/{rid}/input`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of workflow to retrieve the input

get.summary: Return the original input of a specific workflow run

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of workflow to retrieve the input

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The specific run id to target

get.summary: Get workflow trace log data (latest run)
get.description: Returns trace data for the latest run of the given workflow. If trace is stored remotely (S3), fetches and returns the data inline. If trace is local only, returns the local path. To pin a specific run, use `/workflow/{id}/runs/{rid}/trace-log`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of workflow to retrieve trace log

get.summary: Get workflow trace log data for a specific run
get.description: Returns trace data for a pinned workflow run. If trace is stored remotely (S3), fetches and returns the data inline. If trace is local only, returns the local path.

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The specific run id to target

get.description: Returns decoded Temporal history events with optional payload inclusion. First page includes workflow metadata; subsequent pages return events only.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The workflow execution ID

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description: Specific run ID. Required when using pageToken.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of events per page

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Base64 pagination token from previous response

parameters.name: includePayloads
parameters.description: Include decoded input/output payloads in events

parameters.name: wait
parameters.description: Long-poll for a new event when already caught up to the end of history, instead of returning immediately. Bounded server-side; on timeout returns the same page's cursor unchanged with an empty events array so the caller can retry.

runId.description: Resolved run ID. Echo this value as the runId query parameter when fetching subsequent pages.

get.summary: Get paginated workflow execution history for a specific run

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The workflow execution ID

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The specific run id to target

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of events per page

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Base64 pagination token from previous response

parameters.name: includePayloads
parameters.description: Include decoded input/output payloads in events

parameters.name: wait
parameters.description: Long-poll for a new event when already caught up to the end of history, instead of returning immediately. Bounded server-side; on timeout returns the same page's cursor unchanged with an empty events array so the caller can retry.

get.summary: Stream workflow history events via Server-Sent Events
get.description: Opens a persistent SSE connection that delivers Temporal workflow history events in real time. Emits named events: `workflow` (metadata, once), `history` (event batches), `done` (terminal state), `server_error` (post-flush errors). The `done` event carries `{ reason, newRunId? }` where `reason` is the terminal Temporal event type (`WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_COMPLETED`, `WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_FAILED`, `WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_TIMED_OUT`, `WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_CANCELED`, `WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_TERMINATED`, `WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_CONTINUED_AS_NEW`) and `newRunId` is present only when the terminal event chains a follow-on run. `server_error` carries `{ error, message, workflowId, runId }`. Errors before the stream opens are returned as JSON HTTP responses (400/404); once open, failures arrive as a `server_error` event. Supports reconnect via `Last-Event-ID` header or `lastEventId` query param.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The workflow execution ID

parameters.name: includePayloads
parameters.description: Include decoded input/output payloads in events

parameters.name: lastEventId
parameters.description: Resume from this event ID (alternative to Last-Event-ID header)

200.description: SSE stream of workflow history events

get.summary: Stream pinned-run workflow history events via Server-Sent Events
get.description: Same as /workflow/{id}/history/stream but targets a specific run ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The workflow execution ID

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The specific run ID to target

parameters.name: includePayloads
parameters.description: Include decoded input/output payloads in events

parameters.name: lastEventId
parameters.description: Resume from this event ID (alternative to Last-Event-ID header)

200.description: SSE stream of workflow history events

get.summary: Get a specific workflow catalog by ID

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the catalog

workflows.description: Each workflow available in this catalog
Each workflow available in this catalog

get.description: Returns a list of workflow runs with optional filtering by workflow type

parameters.name: workflowType
parameters.description: Filter by workflow type/name

parameters.name: catalog
parameters.description: Filter by catalog ID (scopes runs to a single worker's catalog/session)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of runs to return

post.description: Always targets the latest run; `runId` cannot be pinned for Temporal signal operations.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The workflow id

requestBody.description: Body must contain payload; payload is sent to Temporal

payload.description: The payload sent to the workflow

post.summary: Send a signal to an workflow
post.description: Always targets the latest run; `runId` cannot be pinned for Temporal signal operations.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The workflow id

parameters.name: signal
parameters.description: The signal name

requestBody.description: Body must contain payload; payload is sent to Temporal

payload.description: The payload sent to the signal operation

post.summary: Send a query to an workflow
post.description: Always targets the latest run; `runId` cannot be pinned for Temporal query operations.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The workflow id

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The query name

requestBody.description: Body must contain payload; payload is sent to Temporal

payload.description: The payload sent to the query operation

post.summary: Execute an update on an workflow
post.description: Always targets the latest run; `runId` cannot be pinned for Temporal update operations.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The workflow id

parameters.name: update
parameters.description: The update name

requestBody.description: Body must contain payload; payload is sent to Temporal
